title: Spike: Clarify Account vs Team Entity Relationship in Data Model
description:
Clarify the role of the Account entity in the data model and its relationship to Team. The key question was whether Account and Team have a 1:1 relationship, and which entity holds the Salesforce Org ID.

## Resolved Data Model
After Salesforce authentication, the following model was confirmed:

1. **SalesforceOrganization** — holds the Salesforce Org ID. Created on first authentication if not already present.
2. **User** — created/updated based on Salesforce callback data.
3. **SalesforceAuthToken** — 1:1 relationship with User.
4. **Team** — 1:1 relationship with SalesforceOrganization. Each User owns one Team (via `owner_id`). Users have a 1:many relationship with Teams.
5. **Account** — effectively deprecated and no longer used.

The Account table was replaced by SalesforceOrganization and the Team entity now serves as the primary organizational unit.
acceptance_criteria: SalesforceOrganization entity holds the Salesforce Org ID
Team has a 1:1 relationship with SalesforceOrganization
Each User is the owner of exactly one Team
User has a 1:many relationship with Teams for membership
Account table is deprecated and no longer referenced in new code
data_model_changes: Account table deprecated. Replaced by SalesforceOrganization (holds SF Org ID, 1:1 with Team) and restructured Team entity (owner_id for 1:1 User ownership, 1:many for User membership). SalesforceAuthToken added with 1:1 User relationship.
implementation_notes: The old Account table is no longer in use. After Salesforce OAuth callback, the system creates/updates SalesforceOrganization, User, SalesforceAuthToken, and Team entities in sequence.
comment_summary: Waqar explained the full post-authentication data model: SalesforceOrganization replaces Account, Team has 1:1 with SalesforceOrganization, User owns one Team but can belong to many. Ryan confirmed the Account table is deprecated, which simplifies the model.
